beats.narration: You can stand in a lethal field and feel nothing. Every fact about the radiation comes from an instrument. Meet your only senses.

payload.title: YOUR ONLY SENSES
payload.sub: No human sense detects radiation at the levels that injure.

beats.narration: Three survey-meter families. Ion chamber: accurate, the boundary workhorse. Geiger Mueller: fast, but it saturates. Scintillator: supremely sensitive, it finds lost sources.
The G M trap: in a very intense field pulses pile up, and the meter can fold back to near zero. Quiet can mean danger.

payload.heading: When the meter lies: GM fold-back

beats.narration: On your body, three devices: the pocket dosimeter scores today, the badge certifies the record, the alarming ratemeter guards this second.
Position indicators only hint. The survey meter is the proof the source is home: meter on, in your hand, after every single exposure.
